mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the federal funds rate signals monetary tightening that raises discount rates and compresses equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of instances where FEDFUNDS rises by > 0.5pp in 3 months, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL > 205) signals deteriorating real purchasing power, prompting central bank rate hikes that suppress equity earnings growth and generate negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 50% of instances where CPIAUCSL exceeds 205, or 5d return volatility drops below 1.2% in such periods.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in unemployment (UNRATE < 4.5%) and payroll employment (PAYEMS > 150k) signal overheating labor markets that trigger aggressive rate hikes, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60d SPX returns exceed 1.5% in 40%+ of periods where UNRATE < 4.5% and PAYEMS > 150k, or interaction effect size drops below 0.15 in rolling 3-year regressions.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production (INDPRO > 115) indicates robust manufacturing activity that drives commodity prices higher, generating positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days through sectoral earnings momentum.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 50% of instances where INDPRO exceeds 115, or INDPRO's predictive power for SPX returns falls below 0.05 in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: A synchronized rebound in housing starts (HOUST > 1700) and real estate finance (RSAFS > 370k) signals improving consumer wealth effects, generating positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days through sectoral demand spillovers.
failure_condition: 5d SPX returns fall below 0.5% in 40%+ of periods where HOUST > 1700 and RSAFS > 370k, or interaction term significance drops below p < 0.1 in regressions.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in unemployment (UNRATE < 4.5%) during a recessionary GDP contraction (GDPC1 < 0) signals a labor market paradox where tight labor conditions persist despite weak growth, generating negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60d SPX returns exceed 1.0% in 30%+ of instances where UNRATE < 4.5% and GDPC1 < 0, or interaction effect size falls below 0.10 in rolling 5-year tests.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS < 5%) during a period of rising inflation (CPIAUCSL > 204) signals central bank inaction, generating negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days through real rate uncertainty.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 50% of instances where FEDFUNDS < 5% and CPIAUCSL > 204, or interaction term significance drops below p < 0.05 in regressions.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rebound in industrial production (INDPRO > 114) during a manufacturing recession (GDPC1 < 0) signals sectoral recovery that generates positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days through sectoral earnings rotation.
failure_condition: 5d SPX returns fall below 0.3% in 40%+ of instances where INDPRO > 114 and GDPC1 < 0, or interaction effect size drops below 0.12 in rolling 3-year tests.
